macro_segments.title: Introduction to Stages of Development and the Case of Non-Development

micro_segments.title: Overview of Na'arah and Bogeret stages and the special case of the Ailonit
The Ailonit: A 20-year-old woman without pubic hair and her legal status
The Saris: A 20-year-old man without pubic hair and the laws of Yibum
Discussion of sterility and marriage annulment in Ketubot

macro_segments.title: The Gemara's Reconciliation: Physical Maturity Signs and Age Thresholds

micro_segments.title: The apparent contradiction: nine-year-olds and twenty-year-olds with the same halakhic status
Rav Shmuel's resolution: additional signs of sterility beyond pubic hair
At what age is sterility definitively established: 20, 35, or variable by signs
Rabbi Chiya's medical approach to encouraging pubic hair development

macro_segments.title: Me'et Le'et: Precise Measurement of Years and the Mishnah's Language

micro_segments.title: Rava's statement that the entire chapter follows me'et le'et (time to time)
What does me'et le'et mean: full years vs. entry into a new year
The contrast between Rashi's understanding and other applications of the term
Ula's challenge: why the Mishnah omits 'and a day' for age twenty

macro_segments.title: The Braita of Rabbi Yossi ben Kipar and the 30-Day Standard

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yossi ben Kipar's teaching: 30 days into age 18 or 20 suffices
The Gemara's derivation from 'sheyatzu mimenu shloshim yom'

macro_segments.title: Reconciling the Sources: Me'et Le'et in Sacrifices, Land Sales, and Persons

micro_segments.title: Me'et le'et in the laws of sacrifices: measuring from the animal's birthday
Me'et le'et in house sales and field redemptions: measuring from the sale date
Me'et le'et for a Hebrew slave: six full years before freedom in the seventh
Me'et le'et for sons and daughters: the relevance to Erchin (valuations)

macro_segments.title: The Braita of Rabbi Yossi ben Kipar and the 30-Day Standard (continued)

micro_segments.title: How Rav responds: the braita supports me'et le'et after all

macro_segments.title: Reconciling the Sources: Me'et Le'et in Sacrifices, Land Sales, and Persons (continued)

micro_segments.title: Abaye's clarification: no dispute between Rav Giddal and Rav Yosef

macro_segments.title: The Established Halakha and Rav Zeira's Journey to Eretz Yisrael

micro_segments.title: Rav Yitzchak bar Nachmani: the halakha follows Rabbi Yossi ben Kipar
Rav Zeira's aspiration to learn directly from Rav Elazar in Eretz Yisrael
Rav Elazar's reasoning: the Mishnah's omission of 'and a day' indicates the 30-day standard
Conclusion: the end of the chapter on Yotzei Dofen

macro_segments.title: Broader Implications of Adulthood and Transmission of Halakha

micro_segments.title: The significance of physical maturity as a halakhic criterion for adulthood
The relationship between textual transmission and oral tradition: errors and clarifications
Perspectives on the nature of debates in the Gemara and Halacha Lemoshe Misinai
The meaning of Hilchata: established practice vs. reasoned judgment

macro_segments.title: The Signs of Physical Maturity in Young Women: Upper vs. Lower Development

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah's case: upper signs (breast development) without lower (pubic hair)
Rabbi Meir vs. the Sages: can upper development occur without lower?
The Tanner stages and modern medical comparison to the Mishnah's observations
The Gemara's analysis: why the Mishnah says 'even though it is impossible'

macro_segments.title: Textual Evidence and Interpretation of the Order of Development

micro_segments.title: Yechezkel's verse: 'your breasts became firm and your hair sprouted'
The Sages' reading: upper development proves the lower had already occurred
Rashi's reading: the verse only tells us the sequence, not that both always occur
Universal agreement that pubic hair is the definitive sign

macro_segments.title: The Derivation from Equality of Men and Women in Torah

micro_segments.title: The verse 'a man or woman who commits any sin': equality in law
One sign for men, one sign for women: the parallel application
Why pubic hair and not other indicators for males
Teaching from Yavne: when lower signs are present, upper signs become irrelevant

macro_segments.title: Speculative Observations on Development by Socioeconomic Class and Location

micro_segments.title: Rabban Gamliel: city girls develop lower signs faster due to bathhouse use
Village girls develop upper signs faster due to millstone grinding
Rabbi Shimon ben Elazar: wealthy girls' right side vs. poor girls' left side development
The Gemara's final teaching: typically left side develops before right
Rabbi Chanina's exception: one girl whose right side developed first

topical_tags.term: Ailonit (sterile woman at age 20 without pubic hair)
Saris (sterile man at age 20 without pubic hair)
Yibum and Chalitza (levirate marriage and release)
Na'arah (maiden, 12+ with two pubic hairs)
Bogeret (adult woman post-puberty)
Me'et le'et (time to time; measuring from the event itself)
Physical signs of development (pubic hair and breast development)
Beit Hillel vs. Beit Shammai (age 20 vs. 18 for sterility determination)
Rabbi Eliezer's position (male: 20 years; female: 18 years)
Rav Shmuel bar Rav Yitzchak (additional signs of saris beyond pubic hair)
Rava (entire chapter follows me'et le'et)
Ula (Mishnah's omission of 'and a day' indicates entry into the year suffices)
Rabbi Yossi ben Kipar (30 days into age 18/20 suffices)
Rav Giddal amar Rav (me'et le'et applies to Erchin valuations)
Rav Yosef (me'et le'et applies to Yotzei Dofen)
Abaye (clarification: no dispute between sources)
Rav Yitzchak bar Nachmani (halakha follows Rabbi Yossi ben Kipar)
Rav Zeira (journey to Eretz Yisrael to verify from Rav Elazar)
Rav Elazar (confirms 30-day standard based on Mishnah's language)
Rabbi Meir vs. the Sages (whether upper signs can occur without lower)
Majority of years (35 years as half of 70, threshold for full sterility determination)
Erchin (valuations of vowed persons based on age)
Yotzei Dofen (chapter title; cases of abnormal development)
Halacha Lemoshe Misinai (direct transmission from Sinai)
Rambam's position (most of Torah she-be'al peh is derived, not transmitted directly)
Miktzas hayom k'kulo (a portion of the day counts as a whole day)
Hilchata (established halakha; practice-based ruling)
Torah she-be'al peh (Oral Torah; oral tradition)
Transmission and garbled messages in the Gemara
Tanner stages (modern medical classification of puberty development stages)
Yechezkel's verse on development ('breasts set, hair sprouted')
Equality of men and women in Torah law
Bathhouse use and accelerated development (city girls)
Millstone grinding and upper development (village girls)
Asymmetric development (left vs. right breast development)
Rabbi Chanina ben Achi Rabbi Yehoshua (exception: right developed before left)
